settings.title: Settings
settings.passwordTooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.

login.wordmarkTagline: Sign in to the admin console.

documentSeries.headerHint: Pulled automatically from this organization's Delivery note settings — edit it there, not here.
documentSeries.numberingLocked: Locked — this series can no longer be changed.
documentSeries.numberingHint: Can only be set once — applying it locks the series permanently.
documentSeries.footerPlaceholder: Terms, agreement text, or notes to print at the bottom of every {{kind}}…
documentSeries.confirmBody: You're about to permanently set the numbering series for {{org}} to:
documentSeries.confirmWarning: Once applied, the prefix and starting number can never be changed again — not even by an admin.
documentSeries.confirmCheckbox: I understand this is permanent and cannot be changed later.
documentSeries.primaryEmailHint: One shared Gmail account used to send quotes, invoices, and delivery notes to clients — set it once here, use it from any of the three settings pages.
documentSeries.autoSendEmail: Automatically email this {{kind}} to the client when created
documentSeries.autoSendEmailHint: Uses the primary email above. Requires the client to have an email address on file.

quotes.title: Quotes
quotes.deleteConfirm: Delete the quote for "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
quotes.noPdf: No PDF available — check that a storage connection is configured in Quote settings.

invoices.title: Invoices
invoices.deleteConfirm: Delete the invoice for "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.
invoices.noPdf: No PDF available — check that a storage connection is configured in Invoice settings.
invoices.disclaimer: Basic record-keeping — no payment gateway is connected yet, so "paid" is set manually. Not a certified tax invoice (חשבונית מס); confirm compliance with your accountant before relying on this as the official invoicing system.

deliveryNoteSettings.title: Note template settings
deliveryNoteSettings.logoHint: PNG, JPG · max 2 MB · appears in PDF header
deliveryNoteSettings.subtitle: Subtitle / tagline
deliveryNoteSettings.notePrefixHint: Prefix prepended to the note number (e.g. "DN-10001")
deliveryNoteSettings.startingNumberHint: First note number for this organization
deliveryNoteSettings.termsPlaceholder: Enter the terms text that will appear at the bottom of every printed delivery note…
deliveryNoteSettings.templateMobileNote: The mobile app currently always uses the Classic layout for delivery notes — this setting will apply to it in a future update.
deliveryNoteSettings.autoSendMobileNote: The mobile app doesn't send delivery notes by email yet — this setting is saved for when that's added.

license.lockedFullBody: The license for this installation has not been verified. Contact your Vixor ERP provider to reactivate.
license.lockedAdminBody: The license check is overdue. The admin panel is locked, but the mobile app keeps working for now.
license.invalidKeyFormat: A license key is 64 hexadecimal characters.
license.enterKeyTagline: Enter your license key to activate this installation.
license.noKeyHint: Don't have a key? Contact your Vixor ERP provider.

devices.title: Devices
devices.explanation: Each row is one mobile app installation. Your plan's device limit counts active (non-revoked) devices — revoke one to free up a slot for a new device. Currently {{count}} active.
devices.revokeConfirm: Revoke this device? It will be signed out and blocked from logging in again until re-enabled — this frees up a slot for a new device.
devices.empty: No devices have logged in yet.

files.title: File log
files.clearConfirm: Clear {{scope}}? This deletes the underlying files too — this cannot be undone.
files.partialClearError: Cleared {{deleted}}, but {{failed}} file(s) could not be removed from storage and were kept.
files.emptyBody: Captured documents and photos will show up here once staff start uploading.

orders.title: All orders
orders.deleteConfirm: Delete order "{{name}}"? This also removes the stored PDF. This cannot be undone.

maintenance.title: Maintenance contracts
maintenance.deleteConfirm: Delete this maintenance contract? Future scheduled calls will no longer be created.
maintenance.explanation: A contract auto-creates a real service call for its location on the schedule below — same notifications as a normal call. Runs once daily at 06:00.

templates.title: Naming templates
templates.emptyBody: Define how captured files get named before they're stored.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.legend: Checkboxes in grey = role default. Explicit overrides are saved per-user and override the role.

groups.deleteConfirm: Delete this group? Users in it keep their accounts but lose the group's permissions (falling back to their role default).
groups.explanation: Members of this group get these permissions unless a specific user has their own override set on the Permissions page — a user override always wins over their group.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.description: Each organization's users only see their own organization's calls, locations, and phone book. The logo uploaded here shows as an 80%-opacity background in that organization's mobile app.
organizations.deleteConfirm: Delete this organization? Users in it keep their accounts but lose their organization assignment.

stats.title: Call Statistics
stats.avgResolutionHint: From call creation to closed status

ordersEmail.title: Order intake email
ordersEmail.explanation: Connect the dedicated Gmail inbox that receives supplier purchase orders. The app checks it every 5 minutes for new emails with a PDF attachment, extracts the order date, ordering organization, and PO number from the document, and adds it to the Orders list automatically. This uses a Gmail app password, not the account's real login password — generate one at myaccount.google.com/apppasswords (2-Step Verification must be turned on for that Google account first).
ordersEmail.appPasswordKeepPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep the current one
ordersEmail.notifyOnComplete: Email the finished order when a delivery note is added
ordersEmail.notifyExplanation: Sends the completed PDF (PO + delivery note) to the addresses below as soon as an order is marked done. The subject line is the generated filename (e.g. "2026-07-15 - Company Ltd - רכש 1234 - תמ 5678.pdf").

calls.title: Calls
calls.deleteConfirm: Delete this call permanently? This removes its notes, attachments, and timer history too — files already on storage are not deleted, only the database records.
calls.noSessions: No one has pressed "In progress" yet.

storage.title: Storage connections
storage.synologyHttps: HTTPS (DSM WebDAV default ports: 5005 HTTP / 5006 HTTPS)
storage.ftps: FTPS (encrypted FTP — needs an FTPS-capable server)
storage.emptyBody: Add a local folder, FTP server, or Synology NAS to send captured files to.

storageRouting.title: Storage routing
storageRouting.explanation: Which storage connection each user's uploads go to — required before that user can upload anything from the mobile app.
storageRouting.createOneFirst: Create one on the Storage connections page first.
storageRouting.docTypesExplanation: Where each document type is saved, and how its folder path and filename are built from a template.

warehouse.title: Warehouse
warehouse.noHistory: No history yet for this item.

locations.title: Regions, cities & locations
locations.explanation: One shared directory, used as "Place" on calls/inventory and as "Organization" on phone book contacts. Regions also drive which technicians get notified about a new call (see Users).
locations.deleteRegionConfirm: Delete this region? Cities assigned to it must be removed first.
locations.deleteCityConfirm: Delete this city? Locations assigned to it must be removed first.
locations.mainWarehousesExplanation: These are the default stock locations used when registering new equipment and when searching for equipment while filling out a regular delivery note. Other locations still have their own independent warehouse, but aren't used as a default.
locations.portalExplanation: Generate a link for a location's client to check their service-call status — no login required. Revoking invalidates the link immediately.
locations.revokePortalConfirm: Revoke this client portal link? The current link will stop working immediately.

calendarSync.title: Calendar sync
calendarSync.noOrgSelected: No organization selected for this account.
calendarSync.rotateConfirm: This will invalidate all existing calendar subscriptions. Continue?
calendarSync.selectIcsFile: Please select an .ics file exported from Google Calendar.
calendarSync.subscribeExplanation: Add this URL in Google Calendar / Apple Calendar / Outlook. Events update automatically. Read-only from external apps.
calendarSync.gcalStep4: Paste the URL above → Add calendar
calendarSync.outlookStep1: Calendar → Add calendar → Subscribe from web
calendarSync.autoSyncExplanation: Connect a Google account once — new and changed events on that calendar are pulled into Vixor automatically every ~10 minutes, no manual export needed. One-way: Google → Vixor.
calendarSync.importExplanation: Export your Google Calendar as an .ics file and upload it here. All events will be imported into Vixor. Duplicates are automatically skipped.
calendarSync.howToExport: How to export from Google Calendar:
calendarSync.exportStep3: In the left sidebar click "Import & Export"
calendarSync.exportStep5: Extract the .zip — find the .ics file for the calendar you want
calendarSync.eventsAddedTo: Events will be added to {{org}}'s calendar
calendarSync.notesBody: Events already in Vixor (same UID) are automatically skipped to avoid duplicates. You can re-import the same file safely. Recurring events are imported as individual occurrences.

reports.title: Reports
reports.notTracked: Not tracked by client/location — no location field on this data.

users.deleteConfirm: Delete this user? This cannot be undone.
users.orgCheckboxHint: Check organizations to assign. PRIMARY = home org (data scope). Others = can switch into.
users.globalHint: Global (notified about every call, any region)
users.warehouseTransferHint: Can transfer warehouse equipment between locations
users.noRegionsYet: No regions defined yet — see Locations.
users.emptyBody: Create the first account to let staff sign in from the mobile app.

phonebook.bulkDeleteConfirm: Delete {{count}} contact(s)? This can't be undone.
phonebook.importExplanation: Import selectively from either source — on iPhone, open Contacts, select the contacts to bring over, tap Share, then "Save to Files" (or AirDrop/email to yourself) to get a .vcf file; or connect a Google account directly. Either way, nothing is saved until you pick which contacts to actually import below.
phonebook.noContactsInFile: No contacts found in that file

demoNotice.title: This is a demo installation
demoNotice.intro: You're using Vixor ERP in demo mode to explore its features — not for real business data. Please don't enter real client information, real financial data, or anything you need to keep.
demoNotice.disabled1: Calendar sync, order-intake email, and document-sending email settings
demoNotice.disabled3: New accounts default to limited permissions set by the super-admin
demoNotice.deletionWarning: Everything you create here — calls, orders, quotes, invoices, delivery notes, contacts, locations, warehouse items, vehicles — is automatically and permanently deleted 10 days after it's created.

wizard.welcomeBody: Let's get your installation set up — a few short steps: your profile, the organization, a user group, your first team members, calendar sync, document storage, and how your PDFs look. Everything here can be changed later from Settings.
wizard.profileBody: This is your own account — the person managing the whole installation. Fill in what you'd like others to see; you can leave anything blank and fill it in later.
wizard.orgBody: An organization is the company/business using Vixor — its own users, data, and settings, kept completely separate from any other organization on this installation.
wizard.orgNameRequired: Please enter a name for the organization.
wizard.groupBody: A group is a reusable set of permissions — instead of configuring access for every person one by one, assign them to a group and manage permissions for everyone at once from the Groups page.
wizard.groupNameRequired: Please enter a name for the group.
wizard.usersBody: Create accounts for the people who'll actually use the app day to day. You can add as many as you like now, or skip this and add them later from the Users page.
wizard.calendarBody: Connect a Google Calendar so scheduled calls and maintenance visits show up automatically — this opens in a new tab since it needs to sign in with Google. Come back here (or just close this and continue from Settings) once you're done, or skip it for now.
wizard.storageBody: Where generated PDFs (delivery notes, quotes, invoices) get saved — a local folder on this server, or a remote FTP/SFTP/Synology location. You can add more storage connections later and route different document types to different ones.
wizard.templatesBody: Pick the visual style for quotes, invoices, and delivery notes. You can change this anytime from each document type's settings page, and even switch per document type later.
wizard.doneBody: Your installation is ready to use. You can re-open this wizard anytime from the Settings menu if you want to revisit any of these steps.

appPasswordHelp.title: How to get an App Password
appPasswordHelp.step1: Sign in to the Google account you want to send from.
appPasswordHelp.step2: Turn on 2-Step Verification for that account, if it isn't already on — the App Passwords section won't appear in Google's settings without it.
appPasswordHelp.step4: Create a new app password (any name works, e.g. "Vixor").
appPasswordHelp.step5: Google shows the password as 16 characters, usually grouped like "xxxx xxxx xxxx xxxx".
appPasswordHelp.step6: Copy it straight from Google (spaces and all) and paste it here — the spaces are removed automatically. Just don’t paste your regular Google account password instead.
appPasswordHelp.step7: Try creating a new quote/invoice and check the client's email.

prices.title: Prices
prices.explainer: A reusable catalog of devices and services with standard prices — pick one when adding a line to a quote, invoice, or delivery note to fill in the description and price automatically. You can still edit or replace anything afterward.
prices.name: Name

warehouseWizard.nameBody: Start with the item's name — you can add everything else in the next steps.
warehouseWizard.barcodeBody: Type an existing barcode, or generate a new one automatically.
warehouseWizard.barcodeScanNote: Scanning a physical barcode with the camera is available in the mobile app.
warehouseWizard.detailsBody: Where does this item live, and how is it measured? All optional.
warehouseWizard.priceBody: The reference sale price for this item — used to auto-fill quote/invoice lines. You can leave it blank and set it later.
